steps.title: What ABC precomputes once at startup
steps.narrative: Extra_Truth4VarNPN (in extraUtilMisc.c:643) builds four parallel arrays indexed by 16-bit truth tables (65,536 entries each). For every 4-input function it records which NPN class it lies in, and the canonicalizing permutation+phase that brings it to the class's representative TT. The table is built once when Dec_ManStart runs (called by rewrite, refactor, etc.).

panes.title: Lookup arrays
rows: low 4 bits = input inverters; bit 4 = output negation
canonical TT (lex-smallest representative of the class)

panes.body: 222 classes partition the 65,536 functions. The asserts at extraUtilMisc.c:716 and decMan.c:716 confirm this count.

steps.title: How the table is built (sketch)
steps.narrative:
For each truth table u in [1, 32768) not yet assigned a class:
  1. Allocate a new class index.
  2. For each of the 16 input-phase masks i and 24 permutations k:
    • Compute uPerm = perm(polarize(u, i), k).
    • Mark uPerm and its complement as members of this class.
Each class's orbit is at most 16 × 24 × 2 = 768 NPN-equivalent forms.

steps.title: Concrete example: u = 0x001F
steps.narrative: From the §3 rewrite trace, the cut chosen at f had truth 0x001F (i.e. f = 1 only when input pattern is 0,1,2,3, or 4). Bit i of the truth table is f's value for input pattern i, where pattern bit k = x_k.

steps.title: Lookup result for u = 0x001F
steps.narrative: Read the four arrays at index 0x001F. The values shown here were verified by running print_npn 001F against a built ABC. Note puCanons[0x001F] = 0x001F — this TT is itself the canonical representative of its class. So pPhases and pPerms encode the identity transformation.

entries.Distinct TTs: 384 (i.e. 384 different 16-bit truth tables sit in class 12)
entries.Frequency: 2  (each TT is reached twice across the 768 perm × phase × output combinations)

steps.title: A nearby orbit member: u = 0x010F
steps.narrative: print_npn 0x001F enumerates orbit members. Row 1 of the verbose dump shows: perm = 2 3 1 0, phase = 0 → F = 0x010F. So if rewrite encountered a cut with TT 0x010F, it would still want to use class 12's library subgraphs — but first apply this perm to its 4 fanins.

panes.title: What rewrite does with this
rows: complement fanins by low 4 bits of pPhases[0x010F]
if pPhases bit 4 set, complement the output
evaluate each candidate subgraph in this canonical-coordinate frame
after picking, apply the inverse to wire the new subgraph back into the AIG

steps.title: Storage savings
steps.narrative: Storing per-truth-table subgraphs would need 65,536 × N entries. Storing them per NPN class needs 222 × N — a ~300× compression — at the cost of a few cheap permute/complement operations per use. The same trick is used by map (supergate library) and exact-synthesis caches.
